candidates.evidence_quote: Capital expenditures for new restaurants, including locations under development, were $76.1 million and $61.6 million for the first nine months of fiscal 2024 and 2023, respectively.
We anticipate approximately $180 to $200 million in capital expenditures to support this level of unit development, as well as required maintenance on our restaurants.
We expect to open as many as 22 new restaurants in fiscal 2024 across our portfolio of concepts.

During the first nine months of 2024, we made payments of $6.5 million for deferred consideration and compensation related to the FRC acquisition that was included in cash provided by operating activities.
we issued $345.0 million in aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes (“Notes”), which will mature on June 15, 2026, unless earlier repurchased, redeemed or converted

As of October 1, 2024, we had net availability for borrowings of $236.5 million, based on a $130.0 million outstanding debt balance and $33.5 million in standby letters of credit under the Revolver Facility.
we are subject to financial covenants, as well as to customary events of default that, if triggered, could result in acceleration of the maturity of the Revolver Facility
Common stock dividends of $39.8 million and $40.1 million were paid in the first nine months of fiscal 2024 and 2023, respectively.
We repurchased 0.5 million shares at a cost of $17.5 million, excluding excise tax during the first nine months of fiscal 2024

If any of our third-party vendors experiences a failure that affects a significant aspect of our business, we may experience data loss, increased costs, operational disruption or other harm, any of which could materially adversely affect our financial performance.
Our vendors’ systems have experienced cybersecurity incidents, including credential stuffing attacks in which compromised user credentials were used to breach the systems

in July 2024, we experienced disruptions to our information technology systems as part of the CrowdStrike software update that resulted in global information technology outages, including disruptions to our ability to process customer payments at certain of our restaurants
Total revenues were $865.5 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2024 compared to $830.2 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2023.
Net income and diluted net income per share were $30.0 million and $0.61, respectively, in the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
Comparable restaurant sales at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants increased 1.6% year-over-year in the third quarter of fiscal 2024.
North Italia operating information: Comparable restaurant sales vs. prior year 2 % 8 %
continued strong outperformance in comparable sales and traffic at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants versus the broader casual dining industry
We are capturing market share as evidenced by the continued strong outperformance in comparable sales and traffic at The Cheesecake Factory restaurants versus the broader casual dining industry.
our operators delivering significant improvements in labor productivity, hourly staff and manager retention, and guest satisfaction scores
delivering significant improvements in labor productivity, hourly staff and manager retention, and guest satisfaction scores
significant improvements in labor productivity, hourly staff and manager retention, and guest satisfaction scores
Food and beverage costs 195,306 22.6 % 194,733 23.5 %

The Company recorded pre-tax net income of $2.5 million related to impairment of assets and lease termination income and Fox Restaurant Concepts (“FRC”) acquisition-related expenses.

notes: fiscal_kpis is null for this chunk (KPIs live on chunk_001), so all candidates are event-text grounded.

Chunk has 2 events: 10-Q 0001410578-24-001742 (part 2/2: liquidity/capital-allocation MD&A + updated third-party-vendor risk factor) and 8-K 0001104659-24-112254 (Q3 FY2024 earnings press release EX-99.1). Both reviewed in full, in order.

Deduped across the two events: dividend declaration, share repurchases, new restaurant openings, convertible notes, credit facility availability, and acquisition-related consideration appear in both; one entry kept each.
same_store_sales (flagship) kept brand-unqualified because the flagship brand IS the company legal name (banned in names); north_italia_same_store_sales kept as a separate brand-level driver per R9.
revenue_surprise / eps_surprise use the earnings {metric}_surprise convention for the reported Q3 results.

Skipped (13): unemployment_rate, adverse-weather, pandemic/dining-restriction, landlord/retail-center health, permitting timing, and tax-position items (boilerplate safe-harbor enumeration only); north_italia segment revenue and other_frc segment operating loss (table-only, no narrative attribution; brand comps + openings retained); bakery/third-party bakery and international licensing (descriptive 'About' text, no causal claim); Cheesecake Rewards / delivery-labor regulation (vendor-reliance context only); G&A and D&A lines (flat, no narrative emphasis); total-liquidity (covered by credit_facility_availability); convertible-notes conversion-rate adjustment (instance-bound mechanics of the maturity/notes driver).
Bot is BLIND to other chunks/companies; names coined only from this chunk's text per DriverOntology.md.
